observations: Servo jitters when the DC motor starts or when serial printing occurs; sometimes the Uno resets.
Serial Monitor shows garbled output at 115200; occasionally readable only when I change baud settings.
I2C scanner often reports no devices; when detected, BME280 readings sometimes return NAN.
Pushbutton on D7 reads HIGH intermittently when not pressed (flickers).
LED on D13 became extremely bright; one LED burned out previously.
Uploads fail unless I disconnect the HC-05 from pins 0/1; Serial Monitor cannot open port reliably when HC-05 is connected.
Sketch feels unresponsive — long pauses due to delay() calls.
Touching or moving wires causes sensor readings to change or the system to reset.
When the DC motor runs from its battery pack, the servo moves erratically and readings glitch.
when_it_happens: On startup after a few seconds
Whenever the fan spins up or servo moves
notes: No explicit decoupling capacitors installed; long wire runs (~30 cm) for I2C; servo powered from Arduino 5V; motor battery ground is not tied to Arduino ground.
